title: M9 test support: builder extensions
analysis:
The reify-test-support crate currently has expression builders (literal, binop, comparisons, etc.), a TopologyTemplateBuilder, a CompiledModuleBuilder, fixture functions (bracket, parent_child), mock implementations (MockConstraintChecker, MockConstraintSolver, MockGeometryKernel, etc.), and value/ID constructors. Task 218 requires four categories of extensions:

1. **Trait conformance test helpers**: The TopologyTemplate has `trait_bounds: Vec<String>` and `type_params: Vec<TypeParam>` fields but the builder always sets them to empty vecs. CompiledModule has `trait_defs: Vec<CompiledTrait>` but CompiledModuleBuilder always sets it to empty. We need a TraitDefBuilder for CompiledTrait, extensions to TopologyTemplateBuilder for trait_bounds/type_params, a trait_def() method on CompiledModuleBuilder, and fixture functions that combine traits + structures for conformance testing.

2. **Constraint def fixtures**: Pre-built constraint expression patterns and fixture modules with various constraint configurations (dimensional, logical, multi-constraint) that tests across the codebase can reuse instead of manually constructing constraint expressions.

3. **Determinacy state injection helpers**: The evaluation engine uses PersistentMap<ValueCellId, (Value, DeterminacyState)> for snapshot values. Tests currently construct these manually. A SnapshotValuesBuilder and convenience helpers (determined(), undetermined(), provisional(), auto()) will simplify setting up determinacy states for predicate testing.

4. **Recursive structure fixtures**: Compiled modules with structures that have sub-components referencing the same structure type (tree nodes) or mutual recursion (A→B→A chains), used for testing cycle detection and recursive evaluation.

**Review fix addendum**: Two blocking issues found — (a) bare `matches!` on line 543 silently discards its bool result instead of asserting, (b) CompiledModuleBuilder::build() omits trait_defs from content_hash, causing hash collisions between modules differing only in trait definitions.

steps.description: Write failing test for TraitDefBuilder: trait_def_builder_minimal builds a CompiledTrait with name, empty requirements/defaults/refinements, and valid content_hash
Implement TraitDefBuilder struct with new(name), is_pub(), build() methods. Builder follows the same fluent pattern as TopologyTemplateBuilder. Build computes content_hash from name + members.

Write failing tests for TraitDefBuilder members: trait_def_builder_with_requirement (adds RequirementKind::Param), trait_def_builder_with_default (adds DefaultKind::Constraint), trait_def_builder_with_refinement, trait_def_builder_with_type_param (adds TypeParam with bounds)
Implement TraitDefBuilder methods: requirement(name, kind), default(name, kind), refinement(name), type_param(param). Each returns Self for chaining.

Write failing tests for TopologyTemplateBuilder trait extensions: topology_with_trait_bound verifies template.trait_bounds contains the bound name, topology_with_type_param verifies template.type_params contains the TypeParam
Add trait_bound(name: impl Into<String>) and type_param(param: TypeParam) methods to TopologyTemplateBuilder. Modify build() to use self.type_params and self.trait_bounds instead of empty vecs.
Write failing test for CompiledModuleBuilder trait_def method: module_builder_with_trait_def verifies module.trait_defs is populated after calling .trait_def(compiled_trait)
Add trait_defs field to CompiledModuleBuilder, add trait_def(t: CompiledTrait) method, update build() to use self.trait_defs instead of Vec::new()

Write failing tests for trait conformance fixtures: rigid_trait_module_structure verifies the fixture has 1 trait (Rigid with param mass: Mass, constraint mass > 0kg) and 1 template (Bolt: Rigid with param mass)

Implement rigid_trait_module() fixture in fixtures.rs using TraitDefBuilder and TopologyTemplateBuilder. Creates a module with trait Rigid { param mass: Mass; constraint mass > 0kg } and structure Bolt: Rigid { param mass: Mass = 1kg }.

Write failing test for generic_container_module fixture: verifies module has 2 traits (Rigid, Container<T: Rigid>), 2 templates (Bolt: Rigid, Crate: Container<Bolt>), Container trait has type_param T with Rigid bound
Implement generic_container_module() fixture in fixtures.rs: trait Container<T: Rigid> { param count: Int } with structures Bolt: Rigid and Crate: Container<Bolt>.

Write failing tests for constraint expression helpers: range_constraint(entity, member, min_val, max_val) returns a Vec of two CompiledConstraints (member > min AND member < max), equality_constraint returns one constraint (member == value)

Implement constraint expression helper functions in builders.rs: range_constraint(entity, member, cell_type, min_expr, max_expr) -> Vec<(u32, CompiledExpr)>, equality_constraint(entity, member, cell_type, target_expr) -> CompiledExpr. These compose existing expression builders.

Write failing test for constrained_structure_module fixture: multi_constraint_fixture_structure verifies a module with structure having dimensional constraints (range), logical constraints (boolean condition), and labeled constraints

Implement constrained_structure_module() fixture in fixtures.rs: a Beam structure with width/height params, range constraints on both, a ratio constraint (height > 2*width), and a labeled constraint. Uses constraint expression helpers from step-14.

Write failing tests for determinacy state helpers in values.rs: determined(vcid, value) returns (Value, DeterminacyState::Determined), undetermined(vcid) returns (Value::Undef, DeterminacyState::Undetermined), provisional(vcid, value) and auto_state(vcid) return the corresponding states

Implement determinacy helper functions in values.rs: determined(value) -> (Value, DeterminacyState), undetermined() -> (Value, DeterminacyState), provisional(value) -> (Value, DeterminacyState), auto_state() -> (Value, DeterminacyState). Returns tuples matching the PersistentMap value type used in snapshot_values.

Write failing tests for SnapshotValuesBuilder: builds a HashMap<ValueCellId, (Value, DeterminacyState)> using fluent API — .determined(id, value).undetermined(id).provisional(id, value).auto_val(id).build(). Verify correct values and states.

Implement SnapshotValuesBuilder in values.rs with new(), determined(id, value), undetermined(id), provisional(id, value), auto_val(id), and build() -> HashMap<ValueCellId, (Value, DeterminacyState)>. Uses the tuple helpers from step-18 internally.

Write failing test for recursive_tree_module fixture: verifies a module with TreeNode structure that has param value: Int, optional sub left = TreeNode, optional sub right = TreeNode. Verify sub_components reference same structure_name.

Implement recursive_tree_module() fixture in fixtures.rs: structure TreeNode with param value: Int = 0, sub left = TreeNode, sub right = TreeNode. Both sub-components reference 'TreeNode' as structure_name, creating a self-referencing topology.
Write failing test for mutual_recursion_module fixture: verifies a module with two structures (NodeA with sub child = NodeB, NodeB with sub parent_ref = NodeA) forming a mutual recursion cycle.
Implement mutual_recursion_module() fixture in fixtures.rs: structures NodeA (param a_val: Int, sub child = NodeB) and NodeB (param b_val: Int, sub ref_back = NodeA), creating mutual recursion.

Fix silent assertion in trait_def_builder_with_requirement test (line 543): change bare `matches!(&ct.required_members[0].kind, RequirementKind::Param(_));` to `assert!(matches!(...));` so the RequirementKind check actually asserts instead of silently discarding a bool. This is a test-only fix — no implementation code changes needed.

Write failing test module_builder_trait_defs_affect_content_hash: build two CompiledModules via CompiledModuleBuilder that are identical except one has a trait_def added. Assert their content_hashes differ. This test will fail because build() currently excludes trait_defs from the hash computation.

Fix CompiledModuleBuilder::build() content_hash computation (lines 786-803): add `let trait_def_hashes = self.trait_defs.iter().map(|t| t.content_hash);` and chain it into all_hashes alongside template_hashes, import_hashes, and function_hashes. This ensures modules differing only in trait_defs produce distinct hashes.

design_decisions.decision: TraitDefBuilder follows the same fluent builder pattern as TopologyTemplateBuilder and CompiledModuleBuilder
design_decisions.rationale: Consistency with existing patterns in the crate reduces cognitive load. All three builders use method chaining with terminal .build().

design_decisions.decision: Determinacy helpers return (Value, DeterminacyState) tuples rather than wrapping in a custom struct
design_decisions.rationale: The evaluation engine uses PersistentMap<ValueCellId, (Value, DeterminacyState)> directly. Matching this exact type avoids conversion boilerplate in tests. A SnapshotValuesBuilder provides the ergonomic layer.

design_decisions.decision: Constraint expression helpers are free functions in builders.rs rather than methods on TopologyTemplateBuilder
design_decisions.rationale: Constraint expressions are reusable across different builders and fixtures. Free functions like range_constraint() compose with the existing expression builders (gt, lt, eq, etc.) and can be used standalone for constraint checker tests.

design_decisions.decision: Recursive structure fixtures use sub_component with same structure_name rather than requiring actual type resolution
design_decisions.rationale: TopologyTemplate stores structure_name as a String — it doesn't resolve references at compile time. Recursive references are detected at evaluation/instantiation time, not at template construction. The fixture correctly mirrors how the compiler produces recursive templates.

design_decisions.decision: Trait conformance fixtures model real Reify patterns (Rigid, Container<T>) rather than abstract test names
design_decisions.rationale: Using domain-meaningful names makes tests self-documenting and validates that the builders can represent real-world scenarios from the Reify language spec.

design_decisions.decision: SnapshotValuesBuilder produces HashMap rather than PersistentMap
design_decisions.rationale: PersistentMap (from im crate) is an implementation detail of the evaluation engine. Test setup typically builds a HashMap which can be converted to PersistentMap at the call site. This avoids adding im as a dependency to reify-test-support.

design_decisions.decision: Include trait_defs in CompiledModuleBuilder content_hash computation
design_decisions.rationale: The existing hash intentionally covers all module content (path, templates, imports, functions). Excluding trait_defs was an omission from step-8. Modules differing only in trait definitions must produce distinct hashes for correct change detection and caching.

reuse.how: TraitDefBuilder follows the same new() → method chaining → build() pattern with content hash computation.

reuse.what: Expression builder functions (gt, lt, eq, literal, value_ref, binop, etc.)
reuse.how: Constraint expression helpers compose these existing builders to create common constraint patterns (range, equality, ratio).

reuse.what: Value constructors (mm, deg, newton, etc.) and ID constructors (vcid, cnid)
reuse.how: All new fixtures use these existing helpers for creating values and identifiers rather than constructing Value/ValueCellId directly.
New fixtures use the existing module builder extended with trait_def() method, following the same template+build pattern as bracket_compiled_module().

reuse.what: DeterminacyState and Satisfaction enums from reify-types
reuse.how: Determinacy helpers wrap these existing types into convenient tuple constructors matching the snapshot_values map type.

reuse.what: CompiledTrait, TraitRequirement, RequirementKind, TraitDefault, DefaultKind from reify-compiler
reuse.how: TraitDefBuilder constructs these existing types. No new types needed — only ergonomic builder wrapping.
TopologyTemplateBuilder type_param() and TraitDefBuilder type_param() use these existing types directly.
